commit.message:
feat: replace single-image knot diagrams with multi-step SVG carousel (#264)

* Initial plan

* feat: replace single-image knot diagrams with multi-step SVG carousel

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SVG ampersands and format docs/JSON/TSX

Escape ampersands in Round Turn SVGs (use &) and update SVG comments/titles. Reformat CREDITS.md tables for consistent column alignment and update Wikimedia search table layout. Refactor KnotStepCarousel: add FlatListProps import and strongly-typed onViewableItemsChanged useRef to improve typing. Minor formatting tweak in SectionHeader and compact array formatting in tools.json for improved readability.

* feat: replace placeholder SVGs with Wikimedia Commons WebP images

- KnotStepCarousel now supports both SVG components and static require()
  image sources via a union type (SvgImageSource | StaticImageSource)
- referenceImages.ts updated with a dual-map: svgImages (SVG components)
  and staticImages (require() WebP). New getKnotImage() helper resolves
  either type by key.
- tools.json knot entries reverted from multi-step arrays back to a
  single image key per knot, since Wikimedia sources are composite photos
  (not per-step files). The carousel still works — it will show one
  high-quality image per knot once WebPs are committed.
- CREDITS.md updated with actual Wikimedia Commons file attribution,
  URLs, authors, and licenses for all 9 knots.
- scripts/download-knot-images.js added: run this once locally to fetch
  and convert all 9 Wikimedia Commons images to WebP.

Note: the 9 WebP files (bowline.webp, clove_hitch.webp, etc.) must be
committed to src/assets/images/reference/knots/ after running the script.
Until then, knots fall back to the existing placeholder SVGs.

* fix(tools.json): update knot entries to use single Wikimedia image keys

Each knot entry now uses `images: ["<knot_id>"]` pointing to the
Wikimedia WebP image key. KnotStepCarousel resolves these via
getKnotImage() — WebP when committed, SVG fallback until then.

This replaces the placeholder step arrays (knots_bowline_step1 etc.)
with the real asset keys that map to Wikimedia Commons photos.

* Add bowline WebP, static requires, and Pod tweak

Add bowline.webp and enable static require usage for shipped knot WebP assets so Metro can bundle them while falling back to SVGs when missing. Update referenceImages to use small tryStaticRequire wrapper with inline require() lambdas, remove an unused type import from KnotStepCarousel, and improve the download script (buffer import, https call formatting, better sharp-not-installed message). Also add sharp to package.json and set ALLOW_NON_MODULAR_INCLUDES_IN_FRAMEWORK_MODULES = YES in the iOS Podfile to address non-modular include issues. Update credits formatting to match the new image.

* fix(script): resolve Wikimedia image URLs via Wikipedia API at runtime

Instead of hardcoding upload.wikimedia.org paths that are impossible to
verify from outside the network, look up each knot's lead image URL via
the Wikipedia pageimages API. This guarantees we always get the actual
image used by the article, not a guessed filename.

* Require bowline.webp; add null placeholders

Remove the tryStaticRequire helper and switch to an explicit require for the existing bowline.webp asset. Other wiki knot image entries are left as null placeholders (with comments) to avoid Metro bundle-time failures for files that haven't been downloaded/committed yet. Update the docs to instruct running `node scripts/download-knot-images.js` and clarify that SVG fallbacks are used when WebP images are not available.

* fix(script): correct Prusik article title and add redirects param

- article: 'Prusik knot' → 'Prusik' (the actual canonical Wikipedia title;
  "Prusik knot" is a redirect and the pageimages API returns no image for
  redirect pages unless redirects=1 is passed)
- Add redirects: '1' to the API params so any redirect title is followed
  automatically, making the script more resilient for all knots

* Add reference knot images

Add seven knot reference images to src/assets/images/reference/knots: clove_hitch, overhand_stopper, round_turn_two_half_hitches, sheet_bend, square_reef, taut_line_hitch, and truckers_hitch. Also apply a minor formatting change to the USER_AGENT string in scripts/download-knot-images.js (wrapped across two lines) with no behavioral change intended.

* feat: wire up all 9 knot WebP requires in referenceImages.ts

All WebP files are now committed, so replace the null placeholders with
actual require() calls for the 8 remaining knots.

* Add prusik image and .webp typings

Add new reference asset src/assets/images/reference/knots/prusik.webp and declare '*.webp' in src/types/svg.d.ts so TypeScript can import WebP assets. Require Buffer in scripts/download-knot-images.js to ensure image download/processing works. Also add an eslint-disable-next-line import/order comment for SnowBrushSandals import to avoid a linting error.

* Update scripts/download-knot-images.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address valid Copilot review comments

- package.json: move sharp from dependencies to devDependencies (it's
  only used by the download script, not at runtime)
- scripts/download-knot-images.js: Node 18+ → Node 20+ to match engines
  field in package.json
- CREDITS.md: fix step 4 to reference require() not the nonexistent
  tryRequire() helper
- SettingsModal.tsx: fix attribution text from CC BY-SA 4.0 → CC BY-SA
  3.0 to match the actual license of the bundled Wikimedia assets
- KnotStepCarousel.tsx: improve accessibility label from raw key string
  to "Knot diagram, step N of M"

* Cleanup knot image imports and formatting

Refactor and tidy up knot image handling and related files:

- scripts/download-knot-images.js: add Buffer import and reformat KNOTS entries to multiline objects for readability.
- src/assets/images/reference/knots/CREDITS.md: adjust sheet_bend filename entry.
- src/assets/referenceImages.ts: remove noisy eslint comments and clean import/require ordering/spacing for wiki knot images.
- src/components/KnotStepCarousel.tsx: remove unused KnotImageSource import.
- src/components/SettingsModal.tsx: minor line-wrapping adjustment in attribution text.

These changes are mostly stylistic and to satisfy linters/readability; no functional behavior changes intended.

---------

feat: wire self-send, config validation, rate limiting, meshHealth, dedup, and delivery guards into MeshLink — integration TDD (8 cycles, 72 tests)

Integration wiring:
- Self-send loopback: send(ownId) delivers via messages Flow, bypasses BLE
- Config validation: start() fails with all violations when config invalid

- DedupSet: duplicate reassembled messages rejected at receiver
- Pause FIFO: confirmed queued sends deliver in order on resume
- Callback isolation: subscriber exceptions don't crash library

feat: add broadcast/delivery-ack wire format, rate limiter, delivery tracker, app ID filter, and power mode engine — Phase 5+6 TDD (8 cycles, 56 tests)

WireCodec: broadcast (0x00) with remaining hop count, delivery_ack
(0x06) with recipientId. Encode/decode with golden vector tests.

RateLimiter: per-key sliding window rate limiter. Generic for broadcast
rate limiting (10/min) and other throttling use cases.

DeliveryTracker: single-signal rule — exactly one terminal callback per
messageId (Confirmed or Failed). Late ACKs after tombstone silently
dropped. DeliveryOutcome enum with all failure contexts (NO_ROUTE,
HOP_LIMIT, ACK_TIMEOUT, BUFFER_FULL, PEER_OFFLINE, DELIVERY_TIMEOUT).

AppIdFilter: topic filtering via 16-byte hash. Matching hash accepted,
non-matching silently dropped, null appId accepts all.

PowerModeEngine: 3-tier battery state machine (Performance >80%,
Balanced 30-80%, PowerSaver <30%). Charging forces Performance.
Asymmetric hysteresis: upward immediate, downward delayed 30s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add routing table, presence tracker, dedup set, and routed message wire format — Phase 4 TDD (8 cycles, 49 tests)

RoutingTable (Enhanced DSDV): composite cost routing with primary +
backup routes, higher sequence number preference, route expiry, and
per-neighbor 30% cap for Sybil mitigation.

PresenceTracker: three-state lifecycle (Connected → Disconnected → Gone)
with 2-consecutive-miss eviction hardening.

DedupSet: bounded LRU deduplication set (default 10K capacity) to
prevent duplicate message delivery via multiple paths.

WireCodec: routed_message (0x05) encode/decode with visited-list
(N×16B hashes) for loop prevention and hop limit enforcement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add SACK retransmission, AIMD congestion control, and transfer progress — Phase 3 TDD (8 cycles, 40 tests)

SackTracker: receiver-side selective ACK with 64-bit bitmask, identifies
missing chunks for retransmission.

AimdController: congestion window state machine — grows +2 after 4 clean
rounds, halves on 2 consecutive timeouts, resets on reconnect.

TransferSession: sender-side transfer state machine using AIMD window,
tracks ACKs, computes retransmit set, inactivity timeout support.

MeshLink integration:
- Receiver sends per-chunk ACKs (not just after full reassembly)
- Sender uses TransferSession to track ACKs and retransmit gaps
- New transferProgress Flow emits fraction on each ACK
- VirtualMeshTransport gains dropFilter for simulating packet loss

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(protocol): preserve registry entry order with IndexMap

The RegistryMap inner type was HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> which
has non-deterministic iteration order (Rust's RandomState). When
minecraft:dimension_type entries were sent in random order during
configuration, the hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 in the login packet
would randomly map to the wrong dimension type (e.g. the_nether with
min_y=0 and no skylight instead of overworld with min_y=-64).

This caused intermittent startup issues where:
- Chunks appeared to start from Y=0 instead of Y=-64
- Skybox rendering was wrong
- Blocks were not breakable

The fix:
1. Replace inner HashMap with IndexMap to preserve JSON insertion order
   (matching vanilla's registration order)
2. Add get_registry_entry_index() to look up entry indices by name
3. Replace hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 with dynamic lookup

BREAKING CHANGE: indexmap workspace dep now requires serde feature

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Part A: Remove 3 Java-specific config fields with no Rust equivalent:
- is_jmx_monitoring_enabled (Java JMX monitoring API)
- is_native_transport_enabled (Netty epoll/kqueue toggle)
- is_sync_chunk_writes (Java threading model toggle)

Part B: Extract ~25 hardcoded constants into config structs:
- NetworkTimeoutsConfig: keepalive interval/timeout, login/configuration/write timeouts
- RateLimitConfig: max connections per window, window duration, cleanup interval
- Channel capacities: inbound (128) and outbound (512) channel buffer sizes
- EntityTrackingConfig: per-type tracking ranges (player/animal/monster/misc/projectile/default)
- WeatherConfig: rain and thunder delay/duration min/max values
- World tuning: chunk_cache_size (1024) and max_concurrent_chunk_generations (64)

All constants preserved as documented defaults. Validation ensures keepalive_timeout
>= keepalive_interval, weather min <= max, and positive cache/concurrency values.

Closes R5.18.
